what_it_does: A stdlib-only Python module that checks, controls, and tails logs for registry services by driving systemd/journalctl locally, while also handling manual services and hosted off-box services via HTTP health checks and deploy/logs commands.
observations: Uses only the stdlib (socket, subprocess, urllib.request).
Dispatches behavior on svc['manage']['type']: user-unit, system-unit, manual, or hosted.
User units pass --user to systemctl/journalctl; system units are invoked with sudo -n in control().
Manual services are never started/stopped; the file only reports whether their configured port is open.
Hosted services are probed at a health_url and (re)deployed via a shell command; stop is refused.
port_open() does a TCP connect to 127.0.0.1 on the given port with a short timeout.
is_active() returns one of active, inactive, failed, not-managed, or unknown.

verdict() treats the unit is-active result as authoritative and uses the port only to distinguish RUNNING from active-but-not-listening-yet; drift is inferred only when the port is unique to the service.
control() returns a tuple (ok, message) and truncates stderr on failure.
journal() shows the last 60 lines or follows with -f, and raises for manual services.
standouts: The hosted-service abstraction: remote liveness via health_url, deploy on start/restart, and a host-provided logs command.
verdict() logic that makes unit state authoritative and only uses port as a secondary signal, with explicit shared_ports handling to avoid false positives.
Symbol/label pairs returned for UI display (e.g. RUNNING/▶, FAILED/✖, active (no port yet)/◐).
conventions: Docstrings explain intent and policy (e.g. 'The console drives systemd; it never replaces it').
Behavior is selected by string keys inside the passed svc dict rather than by classes or enums.
Subprocess calls use capture_output=True, text=True and return short stderr snippets on failure.
Constants and defaults are inlined (timeouts, line counts, sudo flag).
concerns: is_active() does not use sudo for system-unit checks, so it may fail or return misleading results if the caller lacks permission to query system units.
_ping_url() and port_open() swallow all exceptions silently, hiding network or configuration errors.
control() and journal() run hosted deploy/logs commands with shell=True using registry-provided strings, which is a command-injection surface if those strings are untrusted.
port_open() calls int(port) without validation; a non-numeric port would raise ValueError.
There is no allow-list or validation for the action argument in control().
verdict() and is_active() can race: the unit state and port state are sampled at different times.
notes: A thin, opinionated ops adapter that delegates to systemd for local units, reports manual processes by port, and pings or redeploys hosted remote functions.
questions: What is the exact schema of the svc dict and where is it defined?
How is the shared_ports frozenset computed by callers?
Why does is_active() not prepend sudo -n for system-unit queries while control() does?
Are health_url, deploy, and logs values trusted, and how are they populated?
What is the intended timeout or failure behavior when systemctl/journalctl hangs?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file's stated purpose is to drive systemd/journald for registry services, and it does so while also covering manual and hosted cases described in the module docstring.
apparent_themes: Delegate to existing tools rather than reimplement process management.
Least-privilege distinction between user and system units.
Drift detection only when evidence is strong (unique port).
intention_signals: To provide a single, minimal interface through which a registry console can check liveness, start/stop/restart, and view logs for services regardless of whether they are local systemd units, manual commands, or remote hosted functions.
novelty: The verdict() heuristic that treats systemd is-active as the authoritative signal and uses port checks only to refine the active state, plus the explicit handling of shared ports to avoid misattributing one service's open port to another.
contribution: A reusable, stdlib-only bridge that normalizes status queries, lifecycle actions, and log tailing across user units, system units, manual services, and hosted remote services, keeping systemd and remote hosts as the actual executors.
summary_for_embedding: A Python stdlib-only operations backend that queries and controls registry services through systemd/journalctl, with dedicated handling for user units, passwordless-sudo system units, manual unmanaged services, and hosted remote services checked by HTTP health URL.
intention_for_embedding: To give the registry console a single honest interface for checking liveness, starting or stopping, and tailing logs without replacing systemd or the remote hosting platform.

symbols.does: GET a remote health URL and return True when the response code is 2xx.
symbols.description: A small urllib-based health probe used for hosted services. It swallows all exceptions and returns False on any failure, making it a simple liveness check with no diagnostic detail.

symbols.does: Return ['--user'] for user-unit services or an empty list otherwise.
symbols.description: A tiny helper that maps the service manage type to the correct systemctl/journalctl scope argument, keeping the rest of the code free of repeated conditionals.

symbols.does: Attempt a TCP connection to 127.0.0.1 on the given port within a short timeout.
symbols.description: A quick socket probe that returns None when no port is configured, True if the connection succeeds, and False on OSError. It is used to refine active states and detect possible unmanaged drift.

symbols.does: Return the active state of a service: active, inactive, failed, not-managed, or unknown.
symbols.description: Queries systemd via systemctl is-active for local units, pings the health URL for hosted services, and returns not-managed for manual services. It is treated as the authoritative liveness signal by verdict().

symbols.does: Produce a human-readable (label, symbol) pair representing the service's current state.
symbols.description: Combines is_active() with port_open() to distinguish RUNNING from active-but-not-listening-yet, and infers unmanaged drift only when the service's port is unique. Hosted and manual services have their own simplified rules.

symbols.does: Start, stop, or restart a service and return an (ok, message) result.
symbols.description: Dispatches to systemctl for local units (using sudo -n for system units), runs the configured deploy command for hosted services, and refuses to act on manual services. It truncates stderr to keep messages concise.

symbols.does: Show or follow recent logs for a service.
symbols.description: Runs journalctl -u for local units, raising for manual services and executing the configured logs command via shell for hosted services. It defaults to the last 60 lines and supports follow mode.
